item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really loved this SCP. It’s funny and well written
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a TV show that consists of a bunch of references to GoIs for no apparent reason. It's effectively just a list of GoIs more than anything of interest. Hard -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think what makes this scip for me is the idea that a small shift in history can have huge repercussions on the timeline. In my head canon the assassination of Jimmy Carter may have had an anomalous component which drew global attention to SCPs, the Foundation and various other GoIs. This in turn dragged all of them out into the light of day and created a much more mainstream acceptance of SCPs and the groups linked to them. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd watch it. It's an SCP TV show. I don't care if it is bad.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, this isn't as good as I remembered. I would like it better if the logs started out with the show having small changes at first, then going through a couple character and genre changes, then slowly devolving into weirdness until it finally becomes about the Foundation, rather than just suddenly having allusions to the Foundation's universe appearing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, this concept is just delightful. I'd love to read about Danny's future adventures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is episode 2, season 10 the events of the SCP: Containment Breach game? It seems eerily similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To note, this was a still from the SyFy movie "Bigfoot" featuring Danny Bonnaduce, cropped to remove a second actor from the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One or several images in this article have been removed due to non-compliance with the Creative Commons license. They may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact apoplexic or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.


This automated message was sent on behalf of apoplexic. Please do not reply to this message or send other messages to this bot. Contact apoplexic in regard to this message. If you believe that you are not the intended recipient of this message, that the image was delivered incorrectly, or have other concerns about this bot, please contact anqxyr.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necroposting here buutt this post made me LOL aaaabbdd has there been any memetic laughs done before?? Like ohh w8 ima try at the discussion thread thanks sorry bye haha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad Eric and a few of the more important SCPs appear in a successful crosstest. Crosstests are fun. +1

EDIT: Christ, I already commented two years ago. Time surely flies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for articulating my feelings. I wanted to like it but I just didn't. And you explained why :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Any uncontained copies of SCP-1257 are to be recovered or destroyed by MTF Mu-53 (“Ebert's Thumb”)."

"Ebert's Thumb"? That's a bit weird but hey, it's pretty clever and as a codename, it works, it's appropriate and its still fairly subtle. As long as it's not overdone, I don't mind this kind of stuff…

"MTF Mu-53 is also tasked to replace any new sources […] in accordance with Protocol Gamma-1257-A (Codename: “Snopes’ Revenge”)"

… aaaaaand ruined it. Nope, sorry, can't take this seriously. There's no way the SCP Foundation administration would go for such a goofy codename. It doesn't help that it fels like this information is literally only there for the sake of a joke, and I'm someone who doesn't even think the odd bit of lolfoundation is necessarily out of character.

And that's really the feel I got from this overall. Decent ideas made ludicrous by adding unnecessary details to a concept that would have worked fine if left alone and not being sure just how goofy it wants to be. I call this the "Steven Moffat Syndrome".

And as other mentioned, it feels like it's been done better many times before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I picture the weekly staff meeting with Gears, Clef, et al. throwing popcorn at the screen.

Not mentioned is if it's the actual SCPs (or badly costumed men in rubber suits) and if any Foundation staff appear as walk-ons/extras.

edit: Didn't mean to reply - meant this to be on it's own. Whoops! (INSERT LAUGH TRACK HERE)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favourite part about the whole article is footnote 4, because you know that the person who felt the need to point out a plot hole is secretly a massive fan of the show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's entirely justified why this is cross-linked with a bunch of famous articles. It's effectively an in-universe fanwork.

If there were a real SCP Foundation TV series, you can bet it'd have episodes about 173 and 682 pretty early on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it though its annoying that a link to SCP-682 was included, would have been better just leaving the title and just suggesting 682 was mentioned, though its an old article so I'll put that to one side.

+1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for Danny Bonaduce

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some bastard trying to molest Eric! That part literally gave me shivers, good job! Eric was always one of my favourite 'characters'…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eric is a character who appears in the backstory of a number of different toy or youth-oriented SCPs, like SCP-168, SCP-066, and SCP-683.

(More here)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the reference…explanation? (A google search reminded me of the terrible TNA angle that bit Danny against Eric Young in a wrestling match that I'd rather not be reminded of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a lot of humourous mainlisters. The only difference between Jokes and mainlisters is that mainlisters have a "Yes, really" aspect, while Jokes are "No, not really", allowing acceptable breaks for humor.

This is fine where it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't break the fourth wall any more than Abe Lincoln reading a biography of himself would.

As for being a joke SCP, while it is humorous, I personally have no difficulty taking it to be a serious part of the canon, and I don't think other people do either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should be a joke SCP, because it kind of breaks the fourth wall, and is undoubtedly humorous.

Plus send, if for nothing other then the funny part with 682

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I really like this. The Wondertainment and "Eric" connection is interesting, too. And, I wonder what the assassination of Jimmy Carter has to do with SCP canon. It's good to see a well made "Crosstest" with the most popular SCPs. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely excellent, well done. Love the concept of the show that just won't accept it was cancelled. Can't quite see why SCP would ever, even in a divergent universe, be open to pop culture though. Seems a bit too far fetched.

I did something similar with SCP-592 inasmuch as the divergent history references SCPs, but this is only worked out by using a computed referential network… One assumes that the book itself only has e.g. "the papier-mâché demon" and "government" not a direct ref to SCP or its subjects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's almost too self-indulgent for my tastes, but overall this is nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good suggestion, adopted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot. I think the idea is an interesting one and that it's executed well here.

My one suggestion would be to change the addendum listing to the format of "Season #, Episode #." That seems to be normal convention I see in referring to episode numbering, plus it makes it easier to 'track' with the eye.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh good. =)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A part of me wants badly to downvote this on conceptual grounds, but the quality is high enough that I cannot do so in good conscience. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that this can reference 682 in a way that improves the overall article is worth an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's Bobble's non-evil cousin. The show is sapient.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly confusing. I think I'm upvoting just for that caption.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, there is SCP-993, Bobble the Clown…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not feeling it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't name anything specific, but I think the whole anomalous broadcast that mentions the foundation by name thing has been done better somewhere. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes a lot of sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually don't much care for this. It gives no sense of a wider world. It is culled from the material on this site, in rough correspondence to popularity. It just seems self-indulgent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am open to suggestions.

EDIT: I unredacted the one for Episode 2, Season 10— seems appropriate that the first episode set at the Foundation would involve the first SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like it, especially the whole "alternate world" bit. I also liked the way the show fluctuates between storylines - it shows this sort of randomness that keeps you intrigued.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While i do like it, i feel it could be better with the blacked out scps filled in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait. His friend's name was Eric.

ERIC.

… Hrrrm, I do wonder if that was intentional, or was just a name pulled from a hat?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like to imagine that it isn't actually broadcasting from an alternate universe, and is rather altering itself in an attempt to suit its target audience.

And since the Foundation is the only people watching it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is probably a rare case where the expungement is intentionally done for humorous effect outside a -J. Though the in-universe explanation is the paranoia of the Intelligence Department, who in my head-cannon are more paranoid than the O5s, just about different things.

PS: I could see the synopsis you wrote as being a scripted episode of SCP-1257. It is that bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Danny Bonaduce as a Foundation agent… 1+.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The SCP itself really isn't that interesting, I'm upvoting for the addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Episode 11, Season 10, “Leaping Lizards”: [REDACTED] SCP-682 [REDACTED].

Episode 11, Season 10, “Leaping Lizards”: Danny dates what he believes to be a beautiful acrobat, only to find out his "love" is SCP-682 in a pink leotard.

Silly expungement aside, pretty interesting idea.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm happy to entertain alternative ways of phrasing the synopsis, as long as it is all in the spirit of 1970's TV racially-themed hijinks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are quite a few television-based SCPs, but I still love seeing new ones. I like the premise here, but I think it's the log of episodes that really makes it. The only two criticisms I have off the top of my head are that it seems like the Foundation is jumping to conclusions, assuming these tapes must be manufactured in another universe, and that the following line seems rather strange to me: "SCP-1257’s original premise had a black man, named Tyler (played by Whitman Mayo), married to a white woman who already had a son by a prior marriage."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really, really fun.

I want to watch this sitcom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still kinda heavy on the redactions, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Again indulging in my attraction to anomalous broadcasts.
